results.title: Ask HN: Why is my Claude experience so bad? What am I doing wrong? | Hacker News
results.content: see anything.  2nd pass, syntax error.  3rd try I ask it to redo everything from scratch. It now has a working slider, but the landscape/portrait is still broken.  4th try, it manages to fix the landscape/portrait issue, but now the issue is that the controls are behind the display so I have to reload the page.  5th try, it manages to fix this issue, but now it is squished again.  6th try, I ask it to try again from scratch. This time it gives me a syntax error.  This is so frustrating. |     |  |  |  --- | | |  |  | kpil 4 months ago  | next (javascript:void(0))   The truth is that there is a lot of hype. You need to be reasonably experienced and guide it.  First, you need to know that Claude will create nonsensical code. On a macro level it's not exactly smart it just has a lot of [...] months ago  | parent | next (javascript:void(0))   I think this reply might have been downvoted for being a bit glib, but the superpowers plugin took my Claude Code experience from mostly frustrating to nearly-magical I’m not a software engineer by training nor trade, so caveats apply, but I found that the brainstorming -> plan writing -> plan execution flow provided by the skills in this plugin helps immensely with extracting assumptions and unsaid preferences into a comprehensive plan—-very similar to the guidance elsewhere in this thread, except automated/guided along by the plugin skills | | | |  |  | LeoPanthera 4 months ago  | prev | next (javascript:void(0))   Are you using plan mode? | | | |  |  | d--b 4 months ago  | prev | next (javascript:void(0))   what language/framework are [...] the stated goal of the project is to produce verified software and the daffy robot still can't seem to understand that the verification part is the critical piece so... it cheats on them so they pass. I had the newest Claude Code (4.6?) look over the tests on the day it was released and the issues it found were really, really bad.  Now, the newest plan is to produce a tool which generates the tests from a DSL so they can't be made to pass and/or match buggy code instead of the clearly defined specification. Oh, I guess I didn't mention there's an actual spec for what we're trying to do which is very clear, in fact it should be relatively trivial to ensure the tests match for some super-human coding machine. | | | |  |  | Multiplayer 4 months ago  | parent | prev | next

results.title: What's your setup to use Claude Code? - Scripting Support
results.content:
My view is a little more optimistic:  
Beginners who use AI tinker around a bit without knowing what they’re doing, but without a true passion, they won’t get far. What you mean is that AI games aren’t going to last long.

Pros, on the other hand, have a tool at their disposal that, even if it currently has many security vulnerabilities, and the other problems you mentioned, I assure you are a matter of time (months, a year at most) and are nothing to worry about.

So my idea is to prepare for the future by learning to live with it now and make the most of it.

Complaining about it now is pointless, because it’s the future. Befriend it, it’s better :wink:

:wink: [...] the only plugin i can recommend is the mcp server i linked above  
skills/plugins to reduce token usage are usually garbage  
best way to keep it is starting new conversations if your task doesnt align with your initial task in that conversation

if you can swing it i’d get a second subscription or something like that specifically for grunt work + playtesting + pentesting, i use claude and minimax respectively but minimax is a bit iffy

Shameless  
This is my roblox developing community 5 years later? We have devolved

Why do you say this?  
Technology has evolved, and so have we devs. I don’t see anything negative about it.  
I don’t think there are people here who have no idea what they’re doing, I mean every professional should ask themselves these questions nowadays [...] So my idea is to prepare for the future by learning to live with it now and make the most of it.

Complaining about it now is pointless, because it’s the future. Befriend it, it’s better :wink:

:wink:

i agree, ai is better than me at scripting & it should be used for everything because i have 6 years of trial and error in luau and know every single up-to-date best practice and the ai doesn’t. you are correct my friend

@AmazingBarnabe Thank you for your response! Can you give me an example of what the whitespace issue is?

Yeah, sure! Here’s an example in one of my games:

image

image748×705 42.4 KB

image

You can see the ‘=’ signs are all aligned. This alignment is what causes the whitespace issue, as there are invisible spaces in between things making them aligned.

results.title: Migrating from Relay to Multiplayer Services (AI not getting it) - Unity Engine - Unity Discussions
results.content:
# Migrating from Relay to Multiplayer Services (AI not getting it)

I rely on AI for all things coding. Claude Sonnet 4.5, via co-pilot, concludes this when trying to migrate.

Yes, I understand now - but the key issue is that the documentation says RelayService.Instance and `AllocationUtils` should exist in the Multiplayer Services package, but they clearly don’t in version 1.1.8.

`AllocationUtils`

This suggests one of two things:

My recommendation: Stay with Relay 1.1.1 for now. Here’s why:

:white_check_mark: It works - Your networking functions properly  
:white_check_mark: It’s stable - You’ve tested it successfully  
:white_check_mark: Deprecation ≠ Broken - It will continue to work for a long time

:white_check_mark:
:white_check_mark:
:white_check_mark: [...] `RelayService.Instance`
`AllocationUtils`

You have the code right in the project, you can quickly look it up or tell Claude to do so:  

image

image384×499 27.8 KB

image

If that’s a problem you can’t solve even with AI, then you’re working yourself into a trap - because you generate code you don’t understand and can’t troubleshoot. It’s a recipe for disaster. You wouldn’t drive a car you built with the help of AI without understanding how cars operate - or at least not at speeds >20 mph unless you’re living dangerous. [...] Keep in mind: unless you’ve set up an MCP server for “fetching” websites or otherwise give Claude access to either the source code that’s in your project, or the precise version of the documentation of the package you are using, it will make incorrect assumptions and conclusion based on what was state of the art at the time of training (typically 6-18 months ago).

Clearly it’s spouting nonsense here - if anything documentation (specifically: scripting/API reference) is lagging behind official releases, never ahead of.

Here is the migration guide from the docs

### Migrate to the Multiplayer Services SDK

results.title: Beyond Claude Code: Multiplayer and Standardized AI Coding - Aviator Blog
results.content:
Aviator

# Beyond Claude Code: Multiplayer and Standardized AI Coding

Here’s what AI coding looks like in engineering orgs now: five developers work on a similar feature using AI tools. Each one opens Claude Code, describes their task and what they want to achieve, and after some back-and-forth, they’re done.

AI tools revolutionized personal productivity, but they’ve also created a new kind of technical debt: fragmented AI-driven development.

Each developer works in isolation with their own AI conversations, receiving different solutions to similar problems. When AI coding sessions end, the reasoning behind the decisions disappears. Junior developers can’t learn from how senior developers use AI. Best practices discovered in one conversation vanish into the void. [...] Antonija Bilic Arar
